elements.title: Book of Hours of Boussu
elements.contents: ... that when the medieval Book of Hours of Boussu (miniature pictured) was restored in 2020, it was discovered that the spine of the book was still sewed with the original thread?

elements.title: a United States airstrike
elements.contents: ... that Yemeni president Ali Abdullah Saleh may have facilitated the killing of a political rival through a United States airstrike?

elements.title: Inna Nikolaevna Leman-Balanovskaja
elements.contents: ... that Russian astrophysicist Inna Nikolaevna Leman-Balanovskaja was sentenced to five years in a Siberian labor camp as a result of false charges?

elements.title: Quarantine II: Road Warrior
elements.contents: ... that several aesthetic and gameplay elements of the video game Quarantine II: Road Warrior, in which the player drives a weaponized hovercab, were inspired by the Mad Max franchise?

elements.title: Hans-Joachim Hacker
elements.contents: ... that Hans-Joachim Hacker worked at a state-owned food processing plant before joining the Volkskammer and Bundestag?

elements.title: Henry Phipps Plaza West
elements.contents: ... that despite being promoted exclusively through word of mouth, New York City's Henry Phipps Plaza West was fully occupied within a year of opening?

elements.title: net-filter coffee
elements.contents: ... that net-filter coffee is named for its spoon-shaped filter, which resembles a hand net?

elements.title: one of his victims
elements.contents: ... that the Minneapolis Police Department initially attributed one of the murders committed by spree killer Andrew Cunanan to one of his victims?

elements.title: March 7th
elements.contents: ... that March 7th first appeared on October 8th?
